steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Calculate area of Circle M
steps.content: With radius 9 mm, the area of Circle M is $$A_M = \pi(9)^2 = 81\pi \text{ mm}^2$$

steps.title: Add both circle areas
steps.content: Circle M has area $81\pi$ mm², and Circle N has area $100\pi$ mm² (given). The total area is $$81\pi + 100\pi = 181\pi \text{ mm}^2$$ That confirms D is correct.
